segments.text: How did Indian kings play holy 400 years ago without plastic balloons? They used a genius piece of ancient design that we
completely forgot about. Today, water balloons can hurt you when they hit you and leave plastic waste everywhere. But the royals of Japur wanted a premium
painless way to throw colors. But how do you create a ball that holds powder,
flies through the air, and shatters softly without hurting anyone? For that,
they used lac, a completely natural tree resin. Craftsmen melt it over hot coals and literally blow it into a paper thin
bubble just like a glass. But a holo bubble is useless for holy. How do you turn this incredibly fragile thin shell into a color bomb without breaking it
into your hands? For that they drop the hot bubble in cold water to instantly harden the shell. [music] Then they carefully fill it with pure gulal powder
and seal it shut. When thrown, these gulal goas burst into beautiful cloud of colors with zero plastic waste. Now that is some amazing design. For more amazing

into a color bomb without breaking it into your hands? For that they drop the hot bubble in cold water to instantly harden the shell. [music] Then they
carefully fill it with pure gulal powder and seal it shut. When thrown, these gulal goas burst into beautiful cloud of colors with zero plastic [music] waste.
